description: Advanced Research Computing (ARC), a division of ITS, offers scientific computing and research consulting services to help implement machine learning and data driven workflows within their projects. These services include expert guidance on data science projects, identifying proper tools, and programming in support of implementing advanced data analytics, machine learning models, and computational techniques to enhance research projects. This service is not for general guidance on use of HPC resource ARC provides.

resource_name: Clinical & Health Research Dissemination & Implementation Science Catalyst (DISC)
description: DISC manages several D&I resources for U-M study teams, including the Implementation Science Network (ISN). The ISN was developed to build a critical mass of faculty, staff, and trainees interested in D&I and enable networking and mentoring opportunities to increase the number of D&I focused research initiatives to reduce the translation gap. DISC also provides access to training resources, a works-in-progress series for investigators, and expert consultations.

description: AI & Digital Health Innovation (AI&DHI) provides researchers across U-M with the tools to develop and implement clinical AI models and AI-based technologies to enhance digital healthcare. resource_name: Michigan Center for Applied and Interdisciplinary Mathematics
description: Founded in 2016, the Michigan Center for Applied and Interdisciplinary Mathematics (MCAIM) serves as a campus-wide hub for applied and interdisciplinary mathematics at the University of Michigan. MCAIM brings together researchers from across campus, including mathematicians, scientists, and engineers to catalyze new collaborations, identify emerging research directions, and advance discoveries that are made possible through shared expertise.

description: The Digital Studies Institute is a center for research and dialogue where faculty, students, and visitors focus their inquiry on technology, digital culture, and social justice. Questions concerning the impacts of technology on race, disability, gender, sexuality, class, power, and identity are foregrounded in courses for our undergraduate minor and graduate concentration; in our annual summer institute; and in a diverse range of public programs. The DSI is also home to the research initiative the DISCO Network.
